findings.message: No example blocks (input→output usage demos) present in any of the 100 audited SKILL.md or prompt template files
No empty/null $ARGUMENTS handling defined; command always runs against current repo — acceptable for a repo-scoped command but undocumented
Glob declared in allowed-tools but not directly invoked — Bash covers all file inspection; inflates the tool surface unnecessarily
All dependencies use ^ semver ranges (e.g. @modelcontextprotocol/sdk: ^1.29.0); minor/patch upgrades are auto-pulled in distributed MCP servers without explicit lockfile review
No model: declaration in any of the 68 Codex adapter SKILL.md files in plugins/; thin adapters rely on canonical skill model but NLPM cannot select tier for direct adapter invocations
All 68 Codex adapter SKILL.md files carry Version: 1.0.0 regardless of canonical skill version; when canonical skills update the adapters show no drift signal and version numbers become meaningless
No model: declaration
'appropriate' in Phase 3 merge strategy description
No model: declaration
No model: declaration

Skills ln-160, ln-140, ln-115, and ln-112 exist in both skills-catalog/ (canonical) and plugins/documentation-pipeline/ (adapter); description text must stay in sync — currently consistent but at risk on next canonical update
No model: declaration
'relevant' in Layer 2 context analysis steps
No model: declaration
'relevant' appears twice in Detection sections — vague quantifier that leaves detection scope undefined

eval "$cmd" in run_check() executes the second argument as a shell command; any caller supplying a malicious second argument achieves arbitrary code execution — template is designed for developer customization so the attack surface is real
No model: declaration; NLPM cannot select appropriate tier for standalone invocations
Missing > **Paths:** header present on all other skills-catalog SKILL.md files; violates skill_contract.md path-resolution rule — agents may fail to locate referenced files when CWD != repo root
No model: declaration
'appropriate' appears in Phase 4 timing section
No model: declaration
Uses 'appropriate' (Phase 2 bottleneck section) and 'relevant' (Phase 3 local check) — vague quantifiers that leave agent behavior undefined
No model: declaration
Worker Invocation section (line 310) references optimization-runtime/cli.mjs but the Runtime Contract section at the top of the same file references dependency-runtime/cli.mjs — one path is stale
No model: declaration
--dangerously-skip-permissions passed to claude CLI for built-in session; bypasses Claude Code permission model — unsafe if the script is run against untrusted project trees
Second --dangerously-skip-permissions in hex-line session invocation — same risk as line 164; both occurrences must be guarded together
git commit --no-gpg-sign --no-verify in checkpoint_worktree() skips signing and pre-commit hooks on benchmark worktree commits; low risk in isolated worktrees but sets a permissive pattern

All 12 prompt templates under skills-catalog/shared/agents/prompt_templates/ have no YAML frontmatter and cannot be indexed by NL artifact scanners; variable contract between review_base.md and mode files is implicit — missing variables produce silent {placeholder} literals in rendered prompts

Missing YAML frontmatter (name: and description:) AND missing output format specification — {output_schema} placeholder has no default schema defined in-file; callers cannot rely on a stable output shape
{output_schema} is an unresolved placeholder — callers must supply the schema at render time but no default or in-file contract is documented; callers cannot rely on a stable output shape
Missing YAML frontmatter (name: and description:) — NL artifact scanner cannot discover or register this template

Mode files use ## header, ## constraints, ## body markers that act as assembly keys for review_base.md but the protocol is undocumented; these are not standard Markdown headings and will confuse unaware editors
